recapPrompt.prompt: What clue most strongly suggests a fixed-size window?

choices.label: The problem says size k
choices.feedback: Correct. Size k usually means the window width stays fixed.

choices.label: The problem asks for the shortest valid section
choices.feedback: Not this one. Shortest valid sections usually use a dynamic window.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain why sliding windows can be faster than brute force.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the window rules
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main sliding-window clues from memory.
hints: Size k suggests a fixed window.
Changing answer lengths suggest a dynamic window.
Shrink when the current window rule is already satisfied or broken and needs fixing.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main sliding-window clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck when windows stay fixed, when they change length, and why shrinking happens.
